stories.title: ‘Only the president knows’: The world awaits Trump’s Iran deadline
stories.summary: As the clock ticks down to President Donald Trump’s Tuesday evening deadline to reach a war-ending deal with Iran, Gulf Arab countries are in the dark. The U.S.’s European allies are clueless. Even people close to the White House are unsure what’s next. And Trump’s  Tuesday morning threat  that “a whole civilization will die tonight” — unless “something revolutionarily wonderful” happens in the scant hours that remain — has some fearing the worst case scenario: that the president would

stories.title: Maga movement splinters as Trump threatens to eradicate a ‘whole civilization’ if Iran doesn’t make deal – live
stories.summary: Tucker Carlson calls Trump strategy ‘vile on every level’ and Marjorie Taylor Greene joins Democrats’ calls to remove president using 25th amendment Sign up for Breaking News US email alerts During a press conference in Budapest with Hungary’s prime minister Viktor Orbán , vice-president JD Vance is asked how the military goals in Iran can be achieved if the US continues its attacks on the country. Vance was also asked about reports about US attacks on Kharg Island. The vice-president said the p

stories.title: Ukraine war briefing: Zelenskyy reiterates truce offer ahead of Orthodox Easter
stories.summary: Ukrainian president says Russia unlikely to accept – ‘for them, nothing is sacred’; Australian police arrest army reservist for joining war. What we know on day 1,504 Ukraine’s president has renewed his offer to Russia of a mutual ceasefire on strikes against energy infrastructure . “If Russia is ready to stop strikes on our energy infrastructure, we will respond in kind,” he said. “This proposal has been conveyed to the Russian side through the Americans.” Volodymyr Zelenskyy offered last week

stories.title: Hungary pipeline false-flag claims swirl as JD Vance makes election intervention
stories.summary: Claims explosives found near pipeline come before election in which PM Viktor Orbán is trailing in most polls Hungary has placed the gas pipeline that straddles the Serbian border under military protection, the prime minister, Viktor Orbán, has said, as accusations of a false-flag operation continued to swirl before a crunch election at the weekend and an official visit on Tuesday from the US vice-president, JD Vance. Orbán travelled to Hungary’s southern border with Serbia on Monday, one day af

stories.title: Italy’s defense minister fears nuclear escalation in Iran
stories.summary: Italian Defense Minister Guido Crosetto sounded the alarm on Tuesday over the risk that the Iran conflict could turn nuclear. “Madness seems to have gripped the world,” Crosetto told the country’s parliament on Tuesday, echoing remarks from an earlier interview with Corriere della Sera in which he warned that “what is already tragic could become even worse.” His comments come amid U.S. President Donald Trump’s increasingly dire threats against Iran , which the United States and Israe

stories.title: Viktor Orbán told Putin ‘I am at your service’ in October phone call
stories.summary: Transcript reportedly details Hungarian leader offering whatever assistance he can to his Russian counterpart Hungary elections explainer Hungary’s Viktor Orbán offered to go to great lengths to help Vladimir Putin, telling the Russian leader “I am at your service” in an October call, it has emerged, prompting further scrutiny of Budapest’s ties to the Kremlin just as JD Vance arrived in the city. Air Force Two landed in Budapest on Tuesday morning carrying the US vice-president and his wife, Us

stories.title: JD Vance accuses EU of ‘interference’ as he visits Hungary to help Orbán win election
stories.summary: US vice-president rails against ‘bureaucrats in Brussels’ interfering in Sunday’s vote as he arrives in Budapest JD Vance has railed against the EU, accusing it of blatantly interfering in Hungary’s upcoming elections, even as the US vice-president said he had travelled to Budapest to “help” Viktor Orbán win Sunday’s vote. Speaking to reporters shortly after landing in Budapest on Tuesday, Vance’s tone was combative as he alleged that the EU was responsible for “one of the worst examples of fore

stories.title: Human skull discovered during Easter egg hunt near Los Angeles
stories.summary: Authorities launch investigation after human remains found at DeForest Park in Long Beach Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox The discovery of a human skull during an Easter egg hunt near Los Angeles has prompted authorities to launch an investigation. At about 5pm on Sunday a family discovered the human remains during an egg hunt at DeForest Park in Long Beach, California , according to local reports. Continue reading...

stories.title: Democrats outraged at Trump’s Iran post: ‘A threat to commit a war crime’
stories.summary: President threatens in Truth Social post to annihilate Iran if government ignores deadline to reopen strait of Hormuz US politics live – latest updates Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email Donald Trump on Tuesday morning threatened to completely annihilate the entirety of Iranian civilization should their government ignore his 8pm ET deadline to reopen the strait of Hormuz. The president’s own words, posted publicly and tied to a specific deadline and set of demands, provide unusual

stories.title: From ‘stink bugs’ to ‘enemies of the people’: how Viktor Orbán blazed a trail for Trump’s media assaults | Amrit Singh
stories.summary: Hungary’s prime minister has conducted a systematic attack on independent media. The parallels with the US are chilling During his state of the nation address earlier this year, Hungary’s prime minister, Viktor Orbán, outlined a chilling vision of the country’s future. Signalling a new level of aggression in his campaign against the truth if he is returned to power in elections on 12 April, Orbán vowed to purge the country of “bought journalists” and “fake civil society organisations”. Media rep

stories.title: Hungary: US dispatches JD Vance to aid Orban reelection bid
stories.summary: The US is deploying Vice President JD Vance to stump for longtime Hungarian PM Viktor Orban, who trails in election polls. Orban is seen as a symbol of the global far-right and has been an inspiration for Donald Trump.

stories.title: Moscow threatens Baltic states, claiming they help Ukraine strike Russia
stories.summary: The Russian foreign ministry issued veiled threats to Estonia, Latvia and Lithuania, accusing them of assisting Ukraine in Kyiv’s campaign against Russia’s oil terminals.

stories.title: Vance accuses EU of ‘foreign interference’ in upcoming Hungarian election while endorsing Orbán – as it happened
stories.summary: US vice-president claims ‘the bureaucrats in Brussels have tried to destroy the economy of Hungary’ … and here they are! JD Vance and Usha Vance off the Air Force Two, welcomed by Hungarian foreign minister Péter Szijjártó as they begin their two-day trip to the Hungarian capital. Continue reading...

stories.title: Stork warning: woman gives birth midair on Jamaica-to-New York flight
stories.summary: Baby was delivered during Caribbean Airlines flight from Kingston to the US; nationality of child to be determined Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox A routine passenger flight from Jamaica landed at New York ’s John F Kennedy international airport with one more person than it took off with after a woman gave birth in midair, potentially setting up a tricky situation over the newborn’s citizenship. The “medical event” occurred on a Caribbean Airlines fl

stories.title: Serbia looks for pipeline sabotage plot culprit as Vance's Hungary visit nears
stories.summary: Serbia has opened a probe into the attempted pipeline sabotage after explosives were found near Kanjiža, as military intelligence suspects a migrant with training, raising the temperature before a key election in Hungary on Sunday.

stories.title: Hungarian Easter tradition sees women doused with water in Hollókő
stories.summary: A centuries-old Easter tradition continues in a Hungarian village, where men pour water over women in a ritual rooted in folklore and springtime customs.

stories.title: Orbán offered help to Putin 'in any way that I can,' reports say
stories.summary: Orbán offered his support to Putin, likening his assistance to that of a mouse helping a lion in a well-known Hungarian fable, according to a phone conversation transcript obtained by news agency Bloomberg.

stories.title: JD Vance backs Orbán's re-election bid in Budapest visit and hits out at EU
stories.summary: US Vice-President JD Vance intervenes in the campaign to give Orbán a ringing endorsement in Budapest.

stories.title: Keressük Szalay-Bobrovniczky Kristóf honvédelmi minisztert, hogy kérdéseket tegyünk fel neki a Magyar Honvédség helyzetéről
stories.summary: A miniszter eddig csak Facebookon reagált Pálinkás Szilveszter százados állításaira, nyilvános fórumon nem jelent meg, ahol újságírók feltehetnék neki a fontos kérdéseket. Ha tudja, merre jár a honvédelmi miniszter, írja meg nekünk!

stories.title: Kijöttek az uniós adatok: ismét Magyarországon drágultak legnagyobb mértékben a lakások
stories.summary: Térképen, melyik országban mekkora volt az áremelkedés.

stories.title: A kegyes oroszlán nem falja fel a nyavalyás férgecske egeret a mesében, amit Orbán idézhetett Putyinnak
stories.summary: A miniszterelnök valószínűleg Ezópusz fabulájára gondolt, amikor az orosz elnökkel telefonált, de az is lehet, hogy a Tolsztoj-féle, kegyesebb változatra gondolt.

stories.title: Trumpot bekapcsolta Vance, de Orbánnak nem hozott mást a biztatáson kívül – az alelnöki látogatás fél napja
stories.summary: Vance előbb elmondta, hogy nem akarja megmondani, kire szavazzunk, később viszont arra biztatott, hogy szavazzunk Orbán Viktorra. Félnapos közvetítés Vance budapesti látogatásáról.

stories.title: Republikon Intézet: Magyar Pétert többen tartják alkalmasabbnak a miniszterelnöki posztra
stories.summary: Az Orbán alkalmassága mellett voksolók viszont magabiztosabbak a válaszukban.

stories.title: Lázár címmel írták meg a magyarországi mágnások hanyatlástörténetét
stories.summary: A családi múltba vezető versek és regények, valamint a szentföldi svédek között megesett csodák. E heti könyvajánlónkban az első női irodalmi Nobel-díjas és a bűnregény hazai felforgatójának műve is szerepel.

stories.title: Vance szerint bárkivel együttműködnek majd, de Orbán Viktor fogja megnyerni a választást
stories.summary: Orbán azt mondta az amerikai alelnökkel közös sajtótájékoztatón, hogy Magyarország továbbra is kész helyt adni egy Moszkva–Washington közti találkozónak. Vance szerint ami Magyarországon történt, az az egyik legrosszabb példája a külföldi beavatkozásnak. Vance pont kapott egy üzenetet Trump közel-keleti követétől, Witkofftól a sajtótájékoztató közben.

stories.title: A nagy drágulás nem tett jót a tojásfogyasztásnak
stories.summary: A drasztikus áremelkedés eredményeként jelentősen csökkent az elmúlt években Magyarországon az egy főre jutó tojásfogyasztás.

stories.title: Beperli az államot Bige László, szerinte Lázár miatt nem kaptak hatósági engedélyt
stories.summary: A Nitrogénművek Zrt. elnök-vezérigazgatója szerint saját forrásból 10 milliárdos beruházást hajtottak végre, de lassan egy éve várják a hatósági engedélyt, hogy használhassák az üzemet.

stories.title: Titokban állami tízmilliárdokat pumpáltak Orbán egyik kedvenc cégébe, amely aztán később hadiipari cégeket vett az államtól
stories.summary: A Direkt36 birtokába került dokumentumok szerint rejtett módon kerültek állami források a 4iG nevű céghez. Ez épp abban az időben történt, amikor a kormány úgy döntött, hogy ennek a vállalatnak adnak el állami védelmi cégeket.

stories.title: „Készen állok arra, hogy azonnal segítsek” – itt a Putyin–Orbán beszélgetés teljes leirata
stories.summary: A magyar miniszterelnök tavaly októberben a Budapestre tervezett amerikai–orosz találkozó kapcsán beszélt az orosz elnökkel, a Bloomberg szerezte meg a beszélgetés leiratát.

stories.title: J. D. Vance: We will cooperate with whoever wins the elections, but Viktor Orbán is going to win
stories.summary: At a joint press conference with the U.S. vice president, Viktor Orbán said that Hungary remains willing to host a meeting between Moscow and Washington.

stories.title: More people consider Péter Magyar better suited for the position of prime minister, survey shows
stories.summary: Those who voted for Orbán being more suitable, however, are more confident in their opinion.

stories.title: Orbán Viktor egy mémgyanús kézmozdulattal árulta el, hogy mit gondol a választás végeredményéről
stories.summary: J. D. Vance magabiztosan kijelentette, hogy Orbán Viktor fogja nyerni a választást, amire a miniszterelnök meglepően reagált.

stories.title: A tíz legnagyobb kínai autóexportőrből már hat belépett a magyar piacra
stories.summary: Az év elején 8,5 százalékra nőtt a magyar piacon a kínai gyártók piaci részesedése, és a következő 12 hónapban még mindig kitarthat a lendületük.

stories.title: Buda Péter: Ne gondolja senki, hogy örökre homályban maradnak ezek a hamis zászlós műveletek
stories.summary: A nemzetbiztonsági szakértő szerint kommunikációval és dezinformációval ágyaztak meg a Török Áramlatnál a merényletkísérletnek, ami az oroszok és a magyar kormány érdekeit szolgálhatta, és nem az ukránokét.

stories.title: 800 fejet levágtak a fideszes trollfarmhidráról, de több száz újat növesztett
stories.summary: A trollhelyzet fokozódik, már Orbán Viktor posztjainak kihangosítására is bevetik a facebookos kamuprofilokat, ami eddig nem volt jellemző.

stories.title: Trump legidősebb fia szerint Orbánra kell szavazni, hogy ne vegyék át a hatalmat Magyarországon a kommunisták
stories.summary: Ha Magyarország elesik, Kelet-Európa túlnyomó többsége is elesik – vezette le Donald Trump Jr.

stories.title: Az Orbán kampánygyűlésén fellépő amerikai alelnök Kossuthot rocksztárnak nevezte, és megmondta, a szavazásnál kire nem lenne jó hallgatni
stories.summary: Összefoglalónk a magyar miniszterelnök és J. D. Vance budapesti gyűléséről, amin Donald Trump is bejelentkezett telefonon.

stories.title: Wáberer György: Április 12-e sorsdöntő dátum. Arról fogsz dönteni, hogy Európához akarsz-e tartozni vagy az oroszokhoz
stories.summary: A volt kormánybiztos videójához Orbán Viktor és Magyar Péter is hozzászólt.

stories.title: Szimbolikus városok, fordítható választókerületek és fideszes bástyák – így néz ki Orbán és Magyar kampányhajrája
stories.summary: A Tisza Párt elnöke még magasabb fokozatra kapcsolt, és az országjárása utolsó napjai már a kétharmad megszerzését célozzák az elemző szerint. A miniszterelnök a Fidesz számára régóta fontos városokba megy el, majd szombaton egy meglepő helyszínen zárja a kampányt: a budai Várban, ami már többször baljós helyszínnek bizonyult a számára.

stories.title: Szijjártó a Bloomberg jegyzőkönyvéről: Mi ez, ha nem titkosszolgálati beavatkozás?
stories.summary: A miniszter nem tudja, honnan származnak a „titkosszolgálati eszközökkel megszerzett telefonlehallgatások, jegyzőkönyvek”, mert nekik nincs ilyen jegyzőkönyvük.

stories.title: Meghalt a román edzőlegenda, aki 12 napja még a fociválogatott kispadján ült
stories.summary: A 80 éves Mircea Lucescu két nappal a törökök elleni vereség után lett rosszul, a kórházban kapott szívrohamot.

stories.title: Gundalf a fiataloknak üzent Magyar Péter váci fórumán
stories.summary: Gundalf, a Tisza egykori informatikusa és a párt utáni kémkedési ügy egyik főszereplője Magyar Péter országjárásán arról beszélt, hogy eljött a pillanat, amikor a hazájukért tehetnek.

stories.title: Félnapos élő műsorban mutatjuk be J. D. Vance érkezését
stories.summary: Itt lesz velünk Buda Péter biztonságpolitikai elemző, Győri Gábor Amerika-szakértő, a Political Capital szakértői, de Orbán Viktor és J. D. Vance megszólalása mellett azt is megmutatjuk, hogyan rúgja be a hetet Magyar Péter.

stories.title: Magyar Péter reggel 7-kor indította a kampány utolsó hetét Ráckevén, Török Gábor a körzet jelentőségéről ír
stories.summary: A Tisza Párt elnöke újra a Ricsi nevű platós kocsi tetejéről beszélt, videóin az látszott, hogy a HÉV-állomáson sokan gyűltek össze.

stories.title: Volt, hogy rossz minőségű követ szállított a Budapest–Belgrádhoz Orbán apjának cége
stories.summary: Jegyzőkönyvek tanúskodnak arról, hogy nem mindig felelt meg az előírásoknak a Gántról érkező zúzott kő.

stories.title: Annyira nem veszi komolyan a pénzügyi világ a szerbiai robbanószerek ügyét, hogy összeomlás helyett pozitívban nyitott a budapesti tőzsde
stories.summary: A befektetők beárazták a szerb–magyar határ közelében talált hátizsákok által jelentett veszélyt.

stories.title: Leszállt Budapesten J. D. Vance, az Egyesült Államok alelnöke
stories.summary: Az amerikai alelnököt Szijjártó Péter fogadta, aki arról beszélt, 35 éve nem volt rá példa, hogy „ilyen magas rangú amerikai vendéget üdvözölhettünk”.

stories.title: Bloomberg: Orbán azt mondta Putyinnak, kész bármit megtenni, mint a kisegér az oroszlánnak
stories.summary: A lap egy októberi telefonhívásról szerzett meg jegyzőkönyvet, amiben Orbán magát egérhez, az orosz vezetőt pedig oroszlánhoz hasonlította.

stories.title: Magyar Péter szerint ha valódi terrorveszély lenne, lemondták volna az amerikai alelnök látogatását
stories.summary: Magyar Péter szerint a szerbiai gázvezeték elleni támadáskísérlet „harmadosztályú operett-előadás volt”.

stories.title: Meghalt Madarász Katalin népdal- és nótaénekes
stories.summary: A magyar nóta és a cigány dal egyik legnépszerűbb előadója 92 éves volt.

stories.title: J.D. Vance arrives in Budapest
stories.summary: The Vice President of the United States was welcomed by Minister of Foreign Affairs and Trade, Péter Szijjártó, who previously noted that "it has been 35 years since “we had welcomed such a high-ranking American guest.”

stories.title: Serbia, Hungary say explosives found at Russian gas pipeline
stories.summary: Serbian President Aleksandar Vucic said detonators and explosives "of devastating power" were found near the Balkan Stream pipeline, which runs through Serbia and provides Russian gas to Hungary.

stories.title: Vagyonvisszaszerzési Hivatal, személyes felelősség, a hatalomátvétel folyamata – a Magyar Péterrel készült interjú legfontosabb pillanatai videón
stories.summary: Hogy nézne ki a hatalomátvétel? Mi lenne a válasza a migrációs kvótára? Hogyan békélne meg a Fidesszel? A Magyar Péterrel készült, közel másfél órás élő interjúnk legfontosabb pillanatai.

stories.title: Magyar Péter azt ígéri, hogy vasárnap ott lesznek minden szavazókörnél, és mindent rögzíteni fognak
stories.summary: A Tisza Párt elnöke szerint Orbán Viktor újra elővette a félelemkártyát a Török Áramlat elleni állítólagos merényletkísérlettel, és harmadosztályú operett-előadásnak nevezte a látottakat.

stories.title: Rendkívüli lehetőség, ne hagyd ki! – ezzel toborozták a DPK-sokat J. D. Vance keddi látogatására
stories.summary: Hétfő délután a Fidesz még nagy erőkkel kereste a résztvevőket, azóta a regisztrációs limit betelt.

stories.title: A magyarok fele szerint a Fidesz elcsalhatja a választást, többen tartanak orosz, mint ukrán beavatkozástól
stories.summary: A Political Capital felkérésére készült kutatás szerint többen számítanak a Tisza győzelmére, de a kormánypárti választók a magabiztosabbak. A közvélemény egyre kevésbé vevő a Fidesz politikai mítoszaira.

stories.title: Elég baj, de a panellakótelepek mutatnak irányt az új lakóparkoknak
stories.summary: Minden érintett tudja, milyen irányba kellene lépni a budapesti lakásépítésekkel, mégsem haladunk arra.

stories.title: Az álmennyezet felett halmokban állt a fekete por – itt vannak a Samsungból kiszivárgott legdurvább fotók és dokumentumok
stories.summary: Súlyos technikai problémák, igénytelenség és a rendkívül laza hatósági kontroll vezetett ahhoz, hogy a gödi Samsung sok dolgozója éveken keresztül rákkeltő anyagokkal teli levegőben végezte a munkáját. Ebben a cikkben bemutatjuk azokat a jegyzőkönyveket, amelyek szerint a hatósági dokumentumoknál is súlyosabb mérgezések történtek a gyárban.

stories.title: Elemző: Szakadék szélén áll a Fidesz, és az RMDSZ is ott van mellette
stories.summary: A Fidesz rendszerének megingása nemcsak Magyarországon, hanem Erdélyben is érezteti a hatását. Kiss Tamás szociológus szerint az RMDSZ olyan helyzetbe sodródott, ahol már nincs igazán jó választás.

stories.title: Orbán Magyar Péternél egy órával később zárja a kampányt szombaton
stories.summary: A miniszterelnök Budapesten, a Tisza Párt elnöke Debrecenben mond beszédet szombat este.

stories.title: Orbán Viktor elengedte a kormányt
stories.summary: De nem azt.

stories.title: Én leszek az, aki az ellenzék jogosítványait védeni fogja – interjú Magyar Péterrel
stories.summary: Nagyon gyorsan feláll és rengeteg dolga lesz a Vagyonvisszaszerzési Hivatalnak, és azért van szükség lehetőleg kétharmadra, hogy a Fidesz kevésbé tudjon mókolni – élőben interjúztunk Magyar Péterrel.

stories.title: Iványi Gábor a Politicónak: Nem engem kéne lecsukni, hanem Orbán Viktort és a bandáját
stories.summary: A lelkész azt mondta a lapnak, hálás volt, amikor 1989-ben egy hosszú hajú fiatalember hazaküldte az oroszokat, és sosem gondolta, hogy majd ő fogja visszahívni őket.

stories.title: In Estonia, rumors are circulating that the city of Narva wants to secede and join Russia. Is it true?
stories.summary: The Estonian city of Narva is on Europe's eastern border, right next to Russia. Recently there have been online rumors that some locals want to secede from Estonia and join Russia. What's really happening?

stories.title: Hungary's government accused of spying on opposition
stories.summary: Prime Minister Viktor Orban's government used the country's intelligence service to spy on the opposition Tisza Party, says investigative platform Direkt36. What does this mean for the upcoming parliamentary election?

stories.title: News outlets falsely report Somaliland called for extradition of Ilhan Omar
stories.summary: Reports, based on X post from unofficial account, follow JD Vance’s accusations and threats of finding ‘legal remedies’ Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox Several news outlets have falsely reported that Somaliland’s government called for the extradition of Ilhan Omar , basing their stories on a post from an X account that does not represent the state despite its claims to the contrary. Fox News, the New York Post, Sinclair Broadcast Group’s the National

stories.title: Iran calls on young people to form human chains around power plants as Trump deadline looms
stories.summary: President Masoud Pezeshkian says 14m people ‘declared their readiness to sacrifice their lives’ for defence of Iran Middle East crisis – live updates Iranians officials called on young people to form human chains around the country’s power plants and people in Tehran stocked up on basic provisions, as the clock ticked down on Donald Trump’s deadline for Iran to open the strait of Hormuz or face massive strikes on civilian infrastructure. Iranian media showed people gathering outside electricity

stories.title: Trump threatens ‘whole civilization will die tonight’ ahead of Iran deadline
stories.summary: President Donald Trump threatened the death of an entire civilization in a Tuesday morning post on Truth Social, the latest violent warning for the Iranian regime ahead of a deadline for  reopening the Strait of Hormuz set to expire at 8 p.m . Tuesday night may well be “one of the most important moments in the long and complex history of the World,”  he wrote . “A whole civilization will die tonight, never to be brought back again,” he wrote. “I don’t want that to happen, but it probab

stories.title: French hostages in Iran head home ahead of Trump ultimatum
stories.summary: PARIS — French President Emmanuel Macron said two French nationals who had been detained in Iran for years left the country just hours before U.S. President Donald Trump’s impending deadline for Tehran to cut a deal with the United States or face civilizational erasure . Cécile Kohler, 41, and Jacques Paris, 72, spent more than three years behind bars in Iran after being convicted of espionage, charges Paris has long said were spurious. The duo, both teachers, were released in November but

stories.title: Trump threatens to wipe out Iran’s ‘whole civilization.’ EU has no comment.
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — The EU has shrugged off Donald Trump’s threats to wipe out the “whole civilization” of Iran , with diplomats and officials signaling they weren’t taking the U.S. president’s remarks literally. Asked to respond to Trump’s post on the Truth Social platform, which referred to the deadline of Wednesday 2 a.m. Brussels time he has given Tehran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, the European Commission said it had “no comment.” A spokesperson for

stories.title: The Stranger review – lustrously beautiful and superbly realised modern take on the Camus classic
stories.summary: François Ozon’s adaptation of the 1942 novella L’Etranger passionately honours the original text while bringing a contemporary perspective to its themes of empire and race A heatstricken reverie of violence and mystery unfolds in this film, a numb ecstasy of the inexplicable, as experienced by a sensitive white European under the unbearable noonday sun. Set in 1940s French Algeria (and filmed in Morocco), François Ozon’s lustrously beautiful and superbly realised monochrome version of Albert Cam

stories.title: ‘We still deserve due process,’ says Cambodian man deported by US to Eswatini
stories.summary: Pheap Rom was one of 15 people sent to prison in African kingdom last year despite completing US sentences A Cambodian man deported by the US said he would have accepted being sent to Cambodia, but instead ended up imprisoned in Eswatini, a country he knew so little about that when he first read the name he thought it was another immigration detention centre in Louisiana. Pheap Rom, who had been convicted of attempted murder, was one of 10 deportees sent to Eswatini by the US in October 2025. Th

stories.title: First Nation asks court to block Alberta referendum on seceding from Canada
stories.summary: Sturgeon Lake Cree Nation has asked a court to halt the separatist push, arguing it would violate their treaty rights A First Nation in Alberta has said that a separatist push for the province to secede from Canada is “consummately irresponsible and dishonourable” and should be shut down, arguing in court that a proposed referendum would violate their treaty rights. A minority of residents of the oil-rich province have long argued that the province’s woes are due to the structure of payments to

stories.title: UK veterans ‘forced to resign’ for being gay launch legal action against MoD
stories.summary: Steven Stewart and Mark Shephard claim in high court that LGBT Financial Recognition Scheme is ‘structurally unfair’ Two veterans who were forced to resign for being gay due to a ban on LGBT personnel in the armed forces have launched legal action against the Ministry of Defence (MoD) over a scheme set up to compensate them. Steven Stewart, 55, and Mark Shephard, 49, who were both “effectively forced to resign” from the military due to their sexuality, and faced “enduring psychological and relat

stories.title: Starmer urged to limit US access to UK bases after ‘dangerous’ Trump threats
stories.summary: Lib Dems, Greens and some Labour MPs demand UK block US from using its airbases for Iran missions Middle East crisis – live updates Keir Starmer is facing increasing pressure to limit US access to British airbases after Donald Trump threatened “a whole civilisation” would die if Iran ignored his demands, comments that Downing Street has not directly criticised. No 10 has allowed US forces to use UK bases only for defensive missions against Iran, such as targeting missile sites, ruling out involv

stories.title: UN chief decries ‘incendiary rhetoric’ after Trump ramps up threats against Iran as deal deadline looms – Middle East crisis live
stories.summary: UN chief warns that deliberate attacks on civilians amount to war crime; Trump once again implores Iran to make a deal to avert attack Trump says ‘a whole civilisation will die’ if Iran ignores demands Democrats outraged at Trump: ‘A threat to commit a war crime’ Here are some of the latest images coming in from the Middle East as the war continues in week six. The Israeli military has just warned the people of Iran not to use trains, saying that doing so “endangers your life” . Dear Citizens, f

stories.title: Iran still refuses to reopen Strait of Hormuz as Trump threatens infrastructure strikes
stories.summary: The Tehran regime has pushed back on the US president's ultimatum to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, as Trump again reiterated his threat of Iran's "complete demolition" by the end of his deadline.

stories.title: Russia provided Iran with intelligence on Israeli energy sites, Ukraine says
stories.summary: Kyiv stated that Moscow is sharing its satellite intelligence with Iran to help Tehran strike Israel’s energy sites, which could cause power grid collapse and mass blackouts.

stories.title: Gunmen open fire near Israeli consulate building in Istanbul
stories.summary: Three gunmen were involved in a shootout with police near the Israeli consulate in Istanbul on Tuesday, leaving one attacker dead and two wounded and in custody, Turkish authorities said. One of the attackers had links to an “organization that exploits religion,” said Turkish Minister of the Interior Mustafa Çiftçi in a statement . He later specified that the three “terrorists” had been “neutralized” during the clash with the police, with one suspect being kil

stories.title: US is ‘using Mexico as a garbage sink’ leading to ‘toxic crisis’, UN expert says
stories.summary: Marcos Orellana, a special rapporteur, found lax environmental standards and lack of oversight allowed pollution to accumulate Revealed: Mexico’s industrial boomtown is making goods for the US. Residents say they’re ‘breathing poison’ Mexico is facing a “toxic crisis” and has become a “garbage sink” for the US, exposing Mexican communities to dangerous pollution, a UN expert has warned. In an interview with the Guardian and Quinto Elemento Lab, an investigative outlet, Marcos Orellana, an enviro

stories.title: Ben Roberts-Smith arrested: former Australian soldier charged with five war crime murders in Afghanistan
stories.summary: Roberts-Smith previously failed in his attempt to sue three newspapers which published allegations he murdered unarmed civilians and bullied comrades Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Australia’s most decorated living soldier, Ben Roberts-Smith , has been arrested at Sydney airport and charged with war crimes. The Australian federal police and the Office of the Special Investigator announced details of the investigation in Sydney on Tuesday after midday. Continue readi

stories.title: #MeToo movement brings wave of harassment claims across Colombia
stories.summary: Female journalists’ accounts of harassment trigger avalanche of allegations reaching as far as government Juanita Gómez was reporting on an international assignment for Caracol, a Colombian television channel in 2015, when an older colleague attempted to forcibly kiss her by inside a lift. She only managed to break free from him by pushing him away several times. Fearing any complaint would come down to the word of a “girl” against that of a senior presenter, she did not report the incident. Con

stories.title: Student claiming RMIT is ‘complicit in genocide’ in social media post faces misconduct action
stories.summary: Student accused of sharing a video of the university’s defence and aerospace research centre with the RMIT Students for Palestine Instagram account Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast An RMIT University student faces potential suspension over a video accusing the institution of being “complicit in genocide” in Gaza , because of its defence and aerospace research centre’s ties to weapons companies. RMIT has argued the

stories.title: Republicans silent as Democrats call on US cabinet to oust Trump over Iran
stories.summary: Lawmakers call for use of 25th amendment after president brazenly threatens to commit war crimes in Iran As Donald Trump unleashes curse-filled threats against Iran, Democrats are raising alarm over his mental stability and calling for his removal from office – while Republicans remain conspicuously silent. Democrats are escalating their rebukes as the 79-year-old president delivers rambling, incoherent speeches , hurls puerile insults at US allies and brazenly threatens to commit war crimes. He

stories.title: Donald Trump says ‘a whole civilisation will die’ if Iran ignores demands
stories.summary: Attacks on Iran increase and Israel tells Iranians to avoid train travel as deadline to reopen strait of Hormuz looms Middle East crisis – live updates Donald Trump has warned that Iran’s “whole civilisation will die tonight” if Tehran did not comply with his demands, as the world braced to see if the president would deliver on his latest threat to order the mass destruction of Iranian power plants and bridges in the absence of a deal by 8pm EDT (1am BST). Iran’s Revolutionary Guards signalled t

stories.title: Iran war: Trump amps up threats ahead of Hormuz deadline
stories.summary: Trump warned a "whole civilization will die tonight" if Iran doesn't open the Strait of Hormuz, adding, however, "maybe something ... wonderful could happen, who knows." Meanwhile, strikes were reported on Kharg Island.

stories.title: Europe mulls the prospect of a NATO without the US
stories.summary: As the US president escalates threats to destroy Iran, will NATO be collateral damage? A former American ambassador says the alliance is "not dead" but "deeply damaged" by trans-Atlantic divisions.

stories.title: Ukraine: Several killed as Russia strikes bus, other targets
stories.summary: Meanwhile, Ukrainian drone strikes on Russia and Russian-occupied regions killed at least five, including a 12-year-old boy. Ukraine has been pushing for a ceasefire during the Orthodox Easter, which is later this week.

stories.title: Taiwan opposition leader visits China for expected meeting with Xi Jinping
stories.summary: Cheng Li-wun said she "gladly accepted" Xi Jinping's invitation and hopes to be a "bridge for peace".

stories.title: Russian strike on Odesa kills three, including child
stories.summary: A Russian overnight attack on Ukraine’s port city of Odesa killed three people, including a child, and left several others injured, authorities said.

stories.title: Russian drone strike on bus stop in central Ukraine kills three and injures 12
stories.summary: The Russian drone targeted a moving bus deliberately as it was pulling in to a stop to allow waiting passengers on board, officials said.

stories.title: Trump says 'whole civilisation will die tonight' if Iran ultimatum expires
stories.summary: Previously, the US president has warned that unless Tehran reopened the Strait of Hormuz, the US would unleash what he called the "complete demolition" of Iran's critical infrastructure overnight on Wednesday.

stories.title: Bahamas police search for US woman who reportedly fell off boat
stories.summary: Her husband told authorities she fell out of their dinghy on Saturday night and was swept out to sea.

stories.title: France's Sarkozy maintains innocence at Libya election funding trial
stories.summary: Sarkozy was sentenced to five years in prison for his alleged part in a scheme to obtain funds from the government of Libya's leader Muammar Gaddafi in exchange for political and diplomatic favours.

stories.title: 'Only the president knows' what he will do on Iran, White House says in statement
stories.summary: Trump had threatened to target Iranian infrastructure unless Tehran agreed a deal to end the war on Tuesday, saying he was "considering blowing everything up and taking over the oil."

stories.title: Anti-war protesters rally outside US embassy office in Tel Aviv
stories.summary: A small group of anti-war protesters gathered outside the US embassy’s Tel Aviv office, calling for an end to the conflict before police moved to disperse the demonstration.

stories.title: Tracking recent US-Israeli strikes on Iranian infrastructure
stories.summary: Attacks have targeted bridges, steel plants and pharmaceutical facilities, verified videos show.

stories.title: Albanese brings forward Singapore trip and speaks with China in bid to shore up petrol shipments to Australia
stories.summary: Petrol prices have stopped falling, despite the federal government’s cut to the fuel excise last week Track Australia’s fuel prices, service station outages and shipments in charts Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Anthony Albanese will fly to Singapore this week – Australia’s biggest source of petrol – as the government mounts an international bid to keep fuel prices from rising. Diesel is getting more expensive a

stories.title: Row over ‘virtual gated community’ AI surveillance plan in Toronto neighbourhood
stories.summary: Rosedale residents considering car licence plate-scanning Flock system in bid to tackle property crime A row has broken out in one of Canada’s wealthiest neighbourhoods over plans to use an AI-powered surveillance system to create the country’s first “virtual gated community” to combat surging property crime. Crime rates in Toronto as a whole are dropping but residents of Rosedale have been left on edge by a sustained rise in home invasions, with robbers targeting the tree-lined neighbourhood at

stories.title: Spanish politicians clash over request to move Picasso’s Guernica
stories.summary: Madrid and Basque government leaders call each other ‘provincial’ in dispute over the artwork A row has broken out between the Madrid and Basque regional governments in Spain over the latter’s request for Guernica, probably Picasso’s most celebrated work, to be housed temporarily in the Guggenheim Museum in Bilbao to mark the 90th anniversary of the bombing of the Basque town. The work has hung in the Reina Sofía museum in Madrid since 1992 and repeated requests for it to be moved to the Basque

stories.title: ‘For leftist Jews, the Bund is a model’: the radical history behind one of Europe’s biggest socialist movements
stories.summary: A new book by Molly Crabapple documents the rise and fall of a revolutionary Jewish party that fought against Zionism and for ‘solidarity across difference’ There is perhaps no more vivid illustration of the moral nadir Israel has reached than the Knesset’s passage, two days before Passover, of a death penalty law that applies only to Palestinians. The measure, whose approval was greeted with tears of joy and the popping of champagne in the legislative chamber, is a concise legal expression of t

stories.title: Ben Roberts-Smith is back in court, now as a defendant. His case reminds us that there are laws even amid war
stories.summary: The former soldier’s previous defamation trial presents the rare situation of there being hours of evidence of his alleged crimes already on the public record For almost every day of his marathon defamation trial, Ben Roberts-Smith VC, sat in the same spot in the federal court. A chair by the window, bathed in sunshine, from where he could glare at witnesses giving evidence. He sits now in a very different position. Continue reading...

stories.title: Max Chandler-Mather says Greens can use ‘progressive populism’ to win voters deserting major parties for One Nation
stories.summary: The new head of the Greens Institute will organise thousands of volunteers for a major survey of economic and social life around Australia Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Max Chandler-Mather says the Greens can use “progressive economic populism” to win over Australians deserting the major parties for One Nation as the firebrand former MP accused the political class of thumbing its nose at the concerns of everyday voters. Chandler-Mather has been named the new execut

stories.title: National Gallery picks Tokyo Olympic stadium architect to design new wing
stories.summary: London gallery to undergo biggest transformation in its 200-year history, with Kengo Kuma’s design called ‘exemplary’ The National Gallery has announced that its largest and most significant transformation in its 200-year history will be designed by the Japanese architect behind Tokyo’s Olympic stadium. The new wing will be designed by Kengo Kuma as part of Project Domani, the gallery’s expansion into art from the 20th and 21st centuries. Its completion will make the National Gallery the only mu

stories.title: Charity cleared after false claims online over migrant welcome project
stories.summary: Watchdog finds allegations against City of Sanctuary UK were misleading after complaint from Tory MP A refugee charity subjected to vicious social media attacks over a migrant welcome project in schools has been cleared of wrongdoing after watchdogs found allegations it encouraged pupils to send Valentine’s Day cards to asylum seekers were misleading and false. City of Sanctuary UK came under fire last year after rumours spread online that under its schools programme, children were being “forced

stories.title: American journalist released a week after being kidnapped in Iraq
stories.summary: Freelancer Shelly Kittleson was reportedly held by Iran-backed militia which says she must now leave country The US journalist Shelly Kittleson, who was kidnapped from a Baghdad street corner last week, has been released, according to an Iraqi official with direct knowledge of the situation. Kittleson was freed in the afternoon, said the official, who spoke to the Associated Press on condition of anonymity because he was not authorized to comment publicly. He did not share her current whereabout

stories.title: Parents charged after toddler snuck into wolf enclosure at Pennsylvania zoo
stories.summary: Parents face child endangerment charge after their kid suffered a minor injury at ZooAmerica in Hersheypark The parents of a toddler who suffered a minor injury at a Pennsylvania theme park zoo after squeezing through a fence near a wolf enclosure and making contact with one of the animals have been charged with endangering the welfare of children, with police accusing them of paying attention to their cellphones at the time. In a news release, police said that the parents both walked about 25ft

stories.title: ‘Italy has the best benefits’: Milan takes on Dubai as home for the super-rich
stories.summary: With the UAE under fire from Iranian missiles, wealthy investors are turning to Italy’s flat-tax haven Just over a month ago, Dubai was the obvious destination for wealthy Britons in search of a new home. Few cities allow you to earn vast sums tax-free and spend them across any number of luxury hotels, restaurants and shops. But as the United Arab Emirates comes under Iranian fire , Dubai’s reputation – in part created by emigrant influencers – as a haven for the global elite is eroding. Super-r

stories.title: Taiwan opposition leader heads to China for 'peace'
stories.summary: Kuomintang's chairwoman Cheng Li-wun hopes to meet Xi Jinping during her visit to China. Her goal, she said, is to "to preserve peace is to preserve Taiwan."

stories.title: Germany news: Minister floats fare-dodging decriminalization
stories.summary: A debate is brewing about whether fare-evasion on trams, buses and trains in Germany should no longer be a criminal offense. Meanwhile, companies are starting to feel the pinch of war in the Middle East. DW has more.

stories.title: India news: Nuclear reactor in Kalpakkam attains criticality
stories.summary: India is advancing its civil nuclear program with its Fast Breeder Reactor at Kalpakkam now able to self-sustain a nuclear chain reaction. In other news, nine police officers have been sentenced to death. DW has more.

stories.title: Why have US-South Africa relations soured?
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump wants to sideline South Africa on the world stage. What is his issue with the country and when did things start going south?

stories.title: One in five young Germans plan to leave the country
stories.summary: A recent study shows that as many as one in five Germans between the ages of 14 and 29 are actively thinking about moving to another country — and 40% plan to do so in the long term.

stories.title: Inside India's courts, AI's growing role sparks concern
stories.summary: The increasing use of artificial intelligence in courtrooms raises worries that the technology may aggravate bias and structural distortions in the legal system.

stories.title: US citizens beat rising energy bills with homegrown power
stories.summary: In times of skyrocketing energy prices, record-breaking heat waves and blackouts, producing electricity at home has never been more appealing.

stories.title: South Korea turns to EU to revive talks with the North
stories.summary: Seoul's unification minister has urged Brussels to act as mediator in North-South discussions, but Pyongyang still seems to show little appetite for interaction.

stories.title: Gunman killed in shootout near Israel's Istanbul consulate
stories.summary: Israel has said it will "not be intimidated" after an apparent failed attack on its consulate in Istanbul. One man was killed who the Turkish government says had links to a terror group.

stories.title: Artemis II: Crew snaps 'Earthset' in throwback to Apollo 8
stories.summary: The Artemis II astronauts photographed the moment when the Earth dips below the moon's horizon, as they prepare to return to our planet's gravitational pull.

stories.title: Australia's most-decorated living soldier charged over alleged war crimes
stories.summary: Ben Roberts-Smith, who denies all wrongdoing, previously lost a landmark defamation case over the alleged murders.

stories.title: French TGV train collides with a truck, killing one and injuring 27
stories.summary: The TGV driver was killed and 27 people were injured in the accident, which occurred at a road crossing on Tuesday morning.

stories.title: Centauros: Greece's anti-drone system that changes 'war of drones' at low cost
stories.summary: The anti-drone system, named after a mythical creature symbolising untamed forces of nature, uses electronic warfare technology to detect and neutralise UAVs without munitions.

stories.title: Iran war and energy supply disruptions accelerate South Caucasus collaboration
stories.summary: When it comes to connecting the continents of Asia and Europe, Azerbaijan and Georgia play a vital role in the transit of energy. The leaders of both countries have outlined how they will work together to broaden the Middle Corridor.

stories.title: Three gunmen 'neutralised' in shooting incident near Israeli consulate in Istanbul
stories.summary: Authorities said a comprehensive investigation is being carried out with law enforcement units under the coordination of the chief public prosecutor's office.

stories.title: Vietnam's leader To Lam strengthens power in unanimous assembly vote
stories.summary: Vietnam's communist party chief is now also the country's president - an unusual concentration of power.

stories.title: Chilean woman accused of Pinochet-era kidnaps loses Australia extradition battle
stories.summary: Adriana Rivas moved from Chile to Australia in the 1970s, working there as a nanny and cleaner.

stories.title: One dead as train travelling 99mph collides with lorry in France
stories.summary: The lorry driver is in custody after the train driver died during the collision in northern France.

stories.title: Trump's Iran ultimatum: When does it expire and how serious is his threat to 'blow up everything'?
stories.summary: US president warns a civilisation could be about to disappear if the regime in Tehran does not respond to his ultimatum after six weeks of war by Tuesday night. What are the possible scenarios? Euronews explains:

stories.title: WHO suspends Gaza medical evacuations after contractor killed by Israeli troops
stories.summary: Israel's military says troops fired at a vehicle driven by the Palestinian because they believed it posed "an immediate threat".

stories.title: Carnival fever hits Lagos as locals celebrate Afro-Brazilian heritage
stories.summary: The centuries-old Fanti Carnival returned to Nigeria on Monday with a burst of colour and spectacle.

stories.title: Over 180 dead or missing in Mediterranean in last 10 days, UN migration agency says
stories.summary: The IOM has previously highlighted the Central Mediterranean as the deadliest migration corridor in the world.

stories.title: Mum of twins who says home was bombed in Lebanon speaks to BBC
stories.summary: More than one million people in Lebanon have been displaced since the start of the war as Israel expands its ground operation there.

stories.title: One gunman killed and two injured in shooting at Israeli consulate in Istanbul
stories.summary: No Israeli diplomats are currently in Turkey and the Istanbul consulate has been empty for the past two-and-a-half years.

stories.title: Greek parliament recommends lifting MP immunity in EU farm subsidy fraud scandal
stories.summary: Nine of the 11 MPs submitted written statements to the committee, while former Rural Development Minister Kostas Tsiaras and MP Notis Mitarakis appeared in person to make their case.

stories.title: Qatar warns Iran war could now 'spiral out of control'
stories.summary: In a series of direct warnings and as the US deadline for Iran approached later on Tuesday, Qatar’s foreign ministry called for “a way of ending the war before it spiralls out of control”, adding that there was still time for diplomacy to prevail, “before it escalates beyond anyone’s control”.

stories.title: Has Artemis II shown we can land on the Moon again?
stories.summary: The Artemis II mission has been near flawless to date, but has the test flight shown Nasa is ready to send humans to the lunar surface?

stories.title: Rapper Offset in hospital after being shot in Florida
stories.summary: The rapper is in a "stable" condition, with two people detained in connection with the incident.

stories.title: US journalist Shelly Kittleson released by Iraqi militant group, official says
stories.summary: Like many freelancers, Shelly Kittleson often worked on a shoestring budget and without the protections afforded by large news organisations to staff.

stories.title: Russia and China veto UN resolution aimed at reopening Hormuz strait as US deadline for deal looms
stories.summary: One-fifth of the world's oil typically passes through the strait and Iran's chokehold during the war has sent energy prices soaring around the world.

stories.title: US journalist Shelly Kittleson to be released after kidnap in Iraq, militia says
stories.summary: Kataib Hezbollah says the release comes on condition that she leave Iraq immediately.

stories.title: Wireless Festival cancelled after Kanye West blocked from coming to UK
stories.summary: West was due to headline the festival in July but drew criticism over past antisemitic comments.

stories.title: Fake Australian, Chinese and Brazilian police stations: BBC goes inside a seized scam compound
stories.summary: Almost nothing was known about the Royal Hill casino until the Thai military took control of it in December.

stories.title: Iran rejects ceasefire proposal with US as war of words deepens
stories.summary: Iran rejects a new 45-day ceasefire proposal, while the US is still expected to respond, as US President Donald Trump delays a threatened military strike tied to the Strait of Hormuz. Mediators are pushing for talks, but tensions remain high on both sides.

stories.title: 'We're sinking deeper': Iranians brace for infrastructure strikes as Trump deadline nears
stories.summary: Ordinary Iranians respond to the US president's threat to destroy Iran's power plants and bridges unless it opens the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Bangladesh launches measles vaccination drive as child death toll passes 100
stories.summary: UN assists in emergency vaccination drive as country battles worst surge in cases in years amid fall in vaccination rates Bangladesh is battling its worse measles outbreak in years, with more than 100 children dead amid a rise in unvaccinated infants. The government, in partnership with the United Nations, has begun conducting an emergency measles-rubella vaccination drive for children across the country, after more than 900 cases were confirmed since March. Continue reading...

stories.title: Artemis II breaks record for farthest human spaceflight
stories.summary: The Orion spacecraft is in its final phase, with NASA saying astronauts have broken Apollo 13's record for traveling the farthest distance from Earth.

stories.title: Vietnam elects Communist Party leader To Lam as president
stories.summary: A China-style mandate for To Lam marks a shift in Vietnam's power structure where party leadership and state presidency were previously held by different people.

stories.title: Australia arrests decorated veteran Ben Roberts-Smith on war crimes charges
stories.summary: Ben Roberts-Smith is a Victoria Cross recipient and had been linked to the murder of unarmed civilians while deployed in Afghanistan.

stories.title: Can small nuclear reactors solve Europe's energy woes?
stories.summary: Supporters say modular reactors are key to meeting energy goals, but critics say they're a costly distraction.

stories.title: How rescue of US airman in remote part of Iran unfolded
stories.summary: The operation to extract him from the ground in hostile territory was hugely complex and involved multiple US government agencies.

stories.title: Little sign of breakthrough as Trump's Iran deadline nears
stories.summary: The US president is in a delicate political position as the final hours to Tuesday's deadline for Iran to open the Strait of Hormuz tick down.

stories.title: China is winning one AI race, the US another - but either might pull ahead
stories.summary: Both sides don't want to let their rival dominate. And the competition may yet be transformed further.

stories.title: Easter festivities muted for Syrian Christians after recent violence
stories.summary: Syrian Christians saw their Easter celebrations confined to church prayers after armed groups attacked the town of Al-Suqaylabiyah in Hama on 27 March. Euronews spoke to the faithful in the country on one of Christianity's most important holidays.

stories.title: UAE tells Euronews ceasefire not enough for Iran war solution, says 'no trust' in Tehran regime
stories.summary: In a briefing to Euronews, the UAE president's diplomatic advisor called for a durable regional security solution beyond a ceasefire with the US as central to the Gulf’s security, as “there is no trust” in the current Iranian regime, but also sees a “more prominent Israeli influence in the Gulf.”

stories.title: Több mint száz műtárgyat ad át az MNB a miskolci múzeumnak
stories.summary: Varga Mihály szerint a jegybanknak az alapfeladatai ellátására kell koncentrálnia, ezért elosztogatják az 1400 műből álló gyűjteményét.

stories.title: „Sebestyén Júlia több gyerekkel is úgy bánt, ahogy velem: erőszakkal, megfélemlítéssel, megalázóan”
stories.summary: A 18 éves műkorcsolyázó, Zsembery Katinka részletesen beszámolt arról, min ment keresztül, amíg az Európa-bajnok Sebestyén Júlia volt az edzője. A rendőrség még tavaly kiskorú veszélyeztetésével vádolta meg az edzőt.

stories.title: Már több mint 151 ezer levélszavazat érkezett be a Nemzeti Választási Irodához
stories.summary: A legtöbb érvényes levélszavazat eddig olyan választóktól érkezett be, akik szerbiai értesítési címet adtak meg.

stories.title: A parancsnok néhai feleségéről nevezné el a Hold egyik kráterét az Artemis II legénysége
stories.summary: Két új, eddig ismeretlen krátert is megfigyeltek a hétfői átrepülés során, az egyiknek az Integrity, a másiknak a Carroll nevet szánják.

stories.title: Lövöldözés tört ki az izraeli konzulátusnál Isztambulban
stories.summary: A török város kormányzója szerint az egyik támadó meghalt, kettőt pedig ártalmatlanítottak.

stories.title: Az Aston Martinnal gázoló férfi személyesen is szeretne bocsánatot kérni az áldozat családjától
stories.summary: Cs. Dávid szerint sok álhír jelent meg a február 14-i balesetről. Az ügyészség már vádat emelt volna ellene, de a bíróság ezt nem fogadta el.

stories.title: A debreceni alpolgármester szerint a tiszás Tárkányi Zsolt egy MI-vel felturbózott hangfelvételt tett közzé
stories.summary: Tárkányi felvételén állítása szerint a fideszes Barcsa Lajos arról beszél, hogyan segíti a Jobbik kampányát a Tisza Párt kárára, Barcsa erre egy másik hangfelvétellel válaszolt.

stories.title: MOK: Súlyos a helyzet a hódmezővásárhelyi és a makói kórházi ellátásban
stories.summary: Sok orvos felmondta az önként vállalt túlmunkáját. Úgy tudjuk, az orvosok többször próbáltak egyeztetni a kórházvezetéssel, de érdemi előrelépés nem történt.

stories.title: 160-nal ütközött egy TGV-vonat egy teherautóval, meghalt a mozdonyvezető
stories.summary: Az észak-franciaországi balesetnek 13 sérültje van.

stories.title: Győri Gábor: Nem venné ki jól magát, ha Vance „csak azért repked egy ilyen hatalmas útra, hogy egy kampány cheerleadere legyen”
stories.summary: A Policy Solutions Amerika-szakértője a Telex műsorában arról is beszélt, hogy milyen kézzelfogható eredménye lehet a látogatásnak.

stories.title: Trump: Egy egész civilizáció fog meghalni ma éjjel, és soha többé nem tér vissza. Nem akarom, hogy ez megtörténjen, de valószínűleg meg fog
stories.summary: Hétfőn az amerikai elnök azt mondta, egész Iránt el tudnák foglalni egy éjszaka alatt, úgy tűnik, ez kedd este lesz.

stories.title: Nem utazhat be az Egyesült Királyságba Kanye West, lemondták a londoni koncertjeit
stories.summary: A brit belügyminisztérium arra hivatkozva utasította el a vízumkérelmét, hogy a „jelenléte nem szolgálná a közjót”.

stories.title: László Róbert: Minden egyes előkerülő puzzle-darabka újabb és újabb apró szög a koporsóban
stories.summary: A Political Capital választási szakértője arról beszélt, hogy milyen hatásuk van a kibeszéléseknek. A Török Áramlat elleni állítólagos merényletkísérletet nagyon gyengének tartja, J. D. Vance látogatása pedig szerinte nem sokat ér a kampányban.

stories.title: Több százmillió dolláros megállapodásokat kötött a 4iG amerikai űripari cégekkel
stories.summary: A Jászai Gellért-féle 4iG már korábban is üzletelt külföldi cégekkel, most négy űr- és védelmi ipari megállapodást jelentett be a tőzsdei vállalat.

stories.title: „Azt választottuk, hogy ne legyenek választások!” – Bödőcs Tibor második regényéből egész estés animációs film készül
stories.summary: „Egy a cirkusz, egy a sátor!” – hangzik el a Mulat a Manézs előzetesében. A szépségversenyt és halálshow-t is felvonultató trailerből egy, a népen saját kérésére harminc éve uralkodó király alakja rajzolódik ki. Gróf Balázs alkotásából megismerhetjük a dobogó szív zsarnokságát.

stories.title: Dobrev Klára arról írt, hogy tiszás politikusok lehetnek érintettek egy brüsszeli kémügyben, a Tisza szerint egy szó sem igaz belőle
stories.summary: A Demokratikus Koalíció kezdeményezi a Parlament Nemzetbiztonsági Bizottságának összehívását.

stories.title: Elnézést kért az egyházközösség, amiért a fóti templomban azt mondta a pap: Gyurcsány Jézus ellensége
stories.summary: A húsvéti misén elhangzottak a nyugalmazott plébános személyes véleményét tükrözik.

stories.title: Választás elleni bűncselekmény gyanúja miatt nyomoz a rendőrség a XVIII. kerületben
stories.summary: Az NVI 414 gyanús kérelmet továbbított a hatóságoknak, mivel számos, közvetítőn keresztül beadott roma nemzetiségi névjegyzékbe vételi kérelmen találtak gyanús aláírásokat.

stories.title: „Ha megmozdul az elsőszavazók tömege, abba beleremeghet a rendszer”
stories.summary: Mozgósító kampánynak indult, de az ötletgazda Fekete-Győr András szerint mozgalom lesz a Félmillió Fiatalból. Influenszerek segítségével olyanokat is szavazásra ösztönöznek, akik kevésbé elkötelezett választók.

stories.title: Mindenki az Artemis II legénységét figyelte, amikor beúszott a képbe egy üveg Nutella
stories.summary: A NASA cáfolta, hogy előre megbeszélt együttműködés keretében jutott volna ekkora hírnév a mogyorókrémnek.

stories.title: Negyvenéves kor körül sikerült tehetségessé válnom – Spiró György a Buksóban
stories.summary: Egy negyvenéves író életközepi és alkotói válságának naplója ez, háttérben a bomló Kádár-rendszerrel és csernobili nukleáris felhővel. Az egyik legszemélyesebb könyvéről beszélünk Spiró Györggyel a Buksóban.

stories.title: Spiró György a zsidóságáról: Gyerekkoromban megkaptam, hogy nem vagyunk kiválasztott nép, és ez jót tett az egómnak
stories.summary: A Buksó legutóbbi adásának vendége Spiró György író volt, akivel új, Repedt kályhámon macska ül (az 1986-os év) című könyvéről beszélgetett Nyáry Krisztián. Ebben a cikkben Spiró néhány fontosabb gondolatát szedtük össze.

stories.title: Itt a Fidesz üzeneteit kihangosító facebookos trollfarm legújabb 324 tagja
Egy egész civilizáció megsemmisüléséről beszélő Trump már nem tudja hova fokozni az Irán elleni fenyegetést, az ultimátumának határideje pedig lejár
stories.summary: Keddről szerdára virradó éjszaka lejár a határidő, amit az amerikai elnök Iránnak adott a Hormuzi-szoros megnyitására és egy megállapodás megkötésére. Trump pokollal és az iráni erőművek és hidak megsemmisítésével fenyeget.

stories.title: Thailand PM urges working from home amid fears over energy crisis
stories.summary: Anutin Charnvirakul encourages measures such as home working and carpooling as country is reliant on oil imports Thailand’s prime minister, Anutin Charnvirakul, has called on the public to conserve energy, urging work-from-home measures and carpooling, as he warned of the impact of the conflict in the Middle East. In a statement posted on social media, Anutin said Thailand was exposed to the crisis because of its reliance on imported oil and gas, and the country could not be complacent. Continue

stories.title: Az Európai Bizottság arra figyelmezteti a tagállamokat, hogy ne adósodjanak el túlságosan az energiaválság miatt
stories.summary: Több ország is beavatkozott a benzin- és dízelárak alakulásába, az EB szerint ezt csak korlátosan szabadna megtenni.

stories.title: Rangos brit irodalmi díjat nyert Moskát Anita
stories.summary: A Brit Tudományos-fantasztikus Szövetség legjobb, fordításban megjelent novella díját vitte el.

stories.title: Letartóztattak egy hősként ünnepelt ausztrál katonát, háborús bűncselekményekkel vádolják
stories.summary: Egy bírói ítélet szerint Afganisztánban megbilincselt gazdát rúgott le a sziklákról, és egy meggyilkolt tálib lábprotézisét használta italtartónak.

stories.title: Megint drágább lesz a Netflix
stories.summary: Több mint 10 százalékkal emelkedik az összes csomag, és a hozzáadható extra tagokért is többet kell majd fizetni.

stories.title: Menekülne a valóságból? Ezeket a színházi előadásokat ajánljuk a választás napjára
stories.summary: Április 12-én az ország szeme a képernyőkre szegeződik majd, de biztosan lesznek olyanok is, akik legalább pár órára kiszakadnának a valóságból, vagy a fikcióban keresnék a választ a valós kérdésekre.

stories.title: J. D. Vance érkezése miatt egy ideig csak gyalog lehetett megközelíteni a repteret
stories.summary: Útlezárások nehezítik meg a közlekedést kedden és szerdán a fővárosban és környékén, érdemes indulás előtt előre tájékozódni a forgalomkorlátozásokról.

stories.title: US pilot rescued as Trump threatens to hit Iran infrastructure in profanity-laden social media post
stories.summary: A US military pilot has been found in Iran after a search-and-rescue mission. Soon after, US President Donald Trump issued a threat to Iranian infrastructure if the Strait of Hormuz remains closed in a profanity-laden social media post.

stories.title: We've gone mad for puzzles. This makes sense – it’s reassuring to have answers in these perplexing times | Joseph de Weck
stories.summary: Our world feels chaotic, confusing and unfair, but puzzles offer clear rules, solvable problems and reward for effort expended Maybe you’ve noticed it too. Everyone seems to have become fixated on puzzle games. In the morning, over coffee, I play Word Wheel on the Guardian app. Over lunch, colleagues compare notes on Tradle , the game where you guess a country from its exports. Which place exports about 45% fish and 50% crustaceans? Greenland. Another friend can’t fall asleep without her nightly

stories.title: When Suzuki met Suzuki: why a Tokyo dating agency is matching couples with the same name
stories.summary: Japan’s ban on married couples having different surnames has prompted an event to highlight people’s reluctance to change their name At the very least, the three men and three women calming their nerves on a Friday evening at a venue in Tokyo know they have one thing in common. Spaced out across booths, they will soon be placed in pairs and given 15 minutes to get to know one another. Continue reading...

stories.title: Weather tracker: Warm March in US leaves snowpack critically low
stories.summary: Concerns about coming wildfire risk, and temperatures also remain high on other side of Pacific where rare tropical cyclone has formed After a historically warm winter across nine states in the US, the first month of meteorological spring again brought exceptionally high temperatures, with numerous states recording new all-time high temperatures in March. The remarkable intensity and longevity of the warmth have left much of the mountain snowpack, a crucial source of water for millions in the Am

stories.title: Russians living in exile cope with grief far from home
stories.summary: Since the start of the war against Ukraine, many Russians living in exile have been unable to return home for security reasons. Three of them told DW how they cope with the loss of loved ones far from their families.

stories.title: Mediterranean sharks are vanishing in a legal void
stories.summary: Along Libya's coast, sharks — including breeding females — are being sold unchecked. With little enforcement and growing financial pressure on fishermen, vulnerable species are spiraling toward extinction.

stories.title: 'We are at the edge of a battlefield': BBC reports near Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: The BBC's Orla Guerin travels to the edge of the critical waterway that Iran has put a stranglehold on.

stories.title: 'I adore her now': Mother learns to live with child's autism in a country with little help
stories.summary: Malawian Martha Ongwane, brought low by her daughter's autism, found a rare support group.

stories.title: Researchers unveil user-friendly robotic arm in Mexico
stories.summary: In Guadalajara, Mexico, researchers created the E-Redi prosthetic arm, a simpler bionic limb using one muscle sensor to control multiple natural movements.

stories.title: OPEC+ to hike crude output: Will it make a difference to oil prices?
stories.summary: The Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries and its allies (OPEC+) has agreed ‌to increase its crude output as the Middle East conflict continues to disrupt oil traffic passing through the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: In Portugal, bikers join Father Avelino for Easter blessing
stories.summary: In Sintra, Father Avelino leads an Easter motorbike parade, taking the traditional compasso blessing to the streets and uniting bikers in faith.

stories.title: Israel strikes Iran's largest petrochemical facility as Trump's Hormuz deadline looms
stories.summary: Earlier on Monday, Iran warned of more devastating retaliation if the US hits civilian targets, as mediators pushed a 45-day ceasefire to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Revellers parade giant phalluses at Japan’s fertility festival
stories.summary: Crowds pack Kawasaki for Kanamara Matsuri, Japan’s colourful fertility festival using phallic shrines to celebrate life and challenge sex taboos.

stories.title: Going abroad: What will Germany's new military service act actually change
stories.summary: Under the new law, German men aged 17 to 45 must obtain authorisation for extended stays abroad. The obligation is not new, but critics say it should have been communicated more clearly.

stories.title: Ukraine and Syria to cooperate on security in unexpected military alliance
stories.summary: Ukraine's Zelenskyy arrived in Damascus in a surprise visit on Sunday, for his first official visit to Syria after the fall of the pro-Russian al-Assad regime.

stories.title: Mitsotakis urges swift EU probe into MPs over Greek farm subsidy fraud
stories.summary: Greek PM Kyriakos Mitsotakis has asked EU prosecutors to swiftly charge MPs over alleged €23m farm subsidy fraud, as resignations and pressure mount.
